subject: Warp 1.0.7W beta
body:
Dear Users,

the upcoming 1.0.7W will focus on performance. In particular, systems with 
multiple GPUs and/or GPUs with plenty of memory will see significant 
increases in processing speed. To achieve this, I had to move to a 
multi-process architecture instead of the multi-threading implemented 
previously. Since the new architecture is harder to debug, I can't hope to 
catch every bug on the limited pool of systems I have for testing.

If any of you feel adventurous and would like to get more performance ahead 
of the final release, I invite you to test the beta version, which you can 
download here: http://deployment.warpem.com/Warp_1.0.7beta.msi

This version can be installed in a separate folder, without uninstalling 
1.0.6 first. Don't forget to copy old BoxNet data.

Some changes:

–SPEED! 415 K2 / 270 K3 movies per hour using 3x 1080 Ti GPUs, and 550+ K2 
movies using 2 processes per GPU (more on that below).

–Lower memory consumption: K3 movies can now be processed on consumer cards 
with less than 16 GB of memory. A 1080 with 8 GB is fine for 40 frame K3 
movies in many scenarios, but a 1080/2080 Ti with 11 GB will give you more 
leeway.

–Multiple processes per GPU: If you think your cards can fit more than one 
movie simultaneously, you can increase the number of processes. To do this, 
close Warp and open "global.settings" in the installation folder with a 
text editor. Change "ProcessesPerDevice" from 1 to the desired number, save 
the file, and start Warp. This may take some experimentation. A Titan RTX 
with 24 GB can easily do 3 K3 movies at once (to roughly double the 
performance), a 1080 Ti with 11 GB can handle 2 K2 movies (and maybe K3 
movies, but I haven't tried).

–Annoying command line windows: When you start processing, a bunch of 
command line windows will appear to show the output of the new processes. 
These will be gone in the final release, but were left in the beta to make 
debugging easier. If Warp crashes and some of these windows remain open, 
close them before you restart Warp. Some of them may contain useful 
information about the crash, though – please post it in this forum thread.

Cheers,
Dimitry
